successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave/leave.service.ts exists and exports ILeaveService interface and LeaveService class with constructor injecting ILeaveRepository, IEmployeeService, IPolicyService, IBalanceService, IAuditService, INotificationService.

src/modules/leave/leave.controller.ts exists and exports handler functions: submitLeaveRequestHandler, approveLeaveRequestHandler, rejectLeaveRequestHandler, cancelLeaveRequestHandler, getLeaveRequestHandler, listLeaveRequestsHandler, each using Zod validation and RBAC via AuthenticatedRequest.

src/modules/leave/leave.routes.ts exists and exports leaveRoutes function that registers POST /leave/requests, PATCH /leave/requests/:id/approve, PATCH /leave/requests/:id/reject, PATCH /leave/requests/:id/cancel, GET /leave/requests/:id, GET /leave/requests on a FastifyInstance with authentication and authorization preHandlers.

POST /leave/requests creates a SUBMITTED leave request with validated input, returns 201, and all domain mutations (request creation, audit record, notification) execute atomically within a single database transaction using pg PoolClient.
PATCH /leave/requests/:id/approve transitions status to APPROVED, deducts balance, records audit, sends notification, all within a single transaction; returns 200 on success.
PATCH /leave/requests/:id/reject transitions status to REJECTED, records audit, sends notification, all within a single transaction; returns 200 on success.
PATCH /leave/requests/:id/cancel transitions status to CANCELLED, restores balance if previously APPROVED, records audit, sends notification, all within a single transaction; returns 200 on success.
All controller handlers catch async errors and map them to appropriate HTTP error responses (400 for validation, 403/404/400 for business errors, 500 for unexpected) satisfying GP-006.
tests/unit/modules/leave/leave.service.test.ts exists with Jest tests covering all service methods, including transaction commit and rollback scenarios, and all tests pass with `npm test`.

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through repository interfaces.
Audit records: All state-changing operations write an audit record.
Input validation: Validate all inputs at API boundaries before processing.
No sensitive data in logs: Never log passwords, tokens, PII, or financial data.
RBAC enforcement: All API endpoints enforce role-based access control.
Error handling: No unhandled promise rejections. All async errors are caught and handled.
Dependency direction: leave -> employee, leave -> policy, leave -> balance, leave -> notification; no reverse dependencies.
Transactional integrity: All mutations in approve, reject, cancel must be atomic within a single database transaction.
outOfScope: any other modules not explicitly listed
